steps.title: Identify the contrast and what it reveals
steps.content: The sentence sets up a contrast: businesses typically promote themselves, but some do not ______ their marketing. The evidence follows: 33% of restaurants skipped a free tool proven to raise revenue by 5%. These restaurants are leaving an effective, available opportunity unused.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘adorn’: Means to decorate or enhance appearance — the passage is about effectiveness, not aesthetics, so this word doesn't fit the context. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘optimize’: Means to make the most effective use of available opportunities — restaurants that skip a free, revenue-boosting tool are clearly not optimizing their marketing. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘endorse’: Means to openly approve or recommend — the passage describes failing to use a tool, not failing to approve of one's own marketing. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘counteract’: Means to actively undermine — the passage implies neglect, not deliberate self-sabotage, which counteract would require. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
